summary: A two-story residential structure with a stepped dark wood roof, wooden plank walls on both floors with regular window openings, stonebrick foundation and trim, and a stonebrick base platform. The building features a symmetrical rectangular footprint with horizontal banding of materials and decorative wooden pillars at corners.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box with stepped gable roof
shape.symmetry: bilateral symmetry along length and width

materials.name: stonebrick
materials.role: foundation, trim bands, and base platform

rebuild_hints: Build a 10x10 rectangular footprint with stonebrick base platform approximately 2 blocks high
Construct two floors of wooden plank walls, each approximately 3-4 blocks tall
Add stonebrick trim bands horizontally between floors and at top of walls
Place wooden pillars at all four corners, extending from base to roof
Install 5 window openings per floor in regular horizontal alignment, using glass panes with dark frames
Create stepped gable roof using dark wood blocks, with 4-5 stepped layers on each side
Ensure bilateral symmetry in all window and structural placements
Add stonebrick slab flooring around the base perimeter
uncertainties: Exact window frame material (appears dark, possibly dark oak or spruce wood)
Interior layout and room divisions not visible
Precise block-by-block roof stepping pattern (appears to step inward 1-2 blocks per layer)
Whether entrance door exists on unseen side
Exact height of each floor (estimated 3-4 blocks per story)
Material of corner pillars (appears to be dark wood, possibly different from wall wood)
